question: Return a table showing monthly new versus repeat ordering customers, with columns order_month, new_ordering_customers, repeat_ordering_customers, and total_orders.
sql_query:
WITH customer_first_order AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', MIN(order_date)) AS first_order_month
    FROM core.orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS order_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cfo.first_order_month = DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) THEN o.customer_id END) AS new_ordering_customers,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cfo.first_order_month < DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) THEN o.customer_id END) AS repeat_ordering_customers,
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM core.orders AS o
JOIN customer_first_order AS cfo ON o.customer_id = cfo.customer_id
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date)
ORDER BY order_month
pandas_sql_query: WITH monthly_customer_orders AS (SELECT strftime(CAST(order_date AS DATE), '%Y-%m') AS order_month, customer_id, COUNT(*) AS orders_in_month FROM orders GROUP BY 1, 2), customer_first_month AS (SELECT customer_id, MIN(strftime(CAST(order_date AS DATE), '%Y-%m')) AS first_order_month FROM orders GROUP BY 1) SELECT m.order_month, SUM(CASE WHEN m.order_month = f.first_order_month THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS new_ordering_customers, SUM(CASE WHEN m.order_month > f.first_order_month THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS repeat_ordering_customers, SUM(m.orders_in_month) AS total_orders FROM monthly_customer_orders m JOIN customer_first_month f USING (customer_id) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
